name: Affinity Plus
description_short: Affinity Plus is a Minnesota-based federal credit union offering full-service banking as a not-for-profit member-owned cooperative with competitive rates, low fees, and digital banking tools.
description_long:
Affinity Plus Federal Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Minnesota members. The organization emphasizes member ownership, where joining requires opening a Membership Savings account with a $10 minimum balance that represents a share in the credit union. This structure differentiates it from traditional for-profit banks, as the majority of profits return to member-owners rather than external shareholders.

The credit union provides comprehensive banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit cards (Visa Premier Select Rewards), mortgages with current promotional closing cost credits ($1,000 off through May 2026), auto loans, personal loans, and educational financing. Digital banking is a core offering, with a mobile app and online platform featuring multi-factor authentication, security alerts, free credit monitoring, and financial wellness dashboards that display external account balances and spending analysis. Members are automatically enrolled in the MyPlus Rewards program, earning points on debit card and credit card purchases.

Affinity Plus distinguishes itself through several member-centric features: membership eligibility based on common bonds (residence, employment, education, or family connections), transparent fee structures marketed as "simple" with no hidden charges, and multi-language support including Spanish. The organization also demonstrates community commitment through partnerships like the Affinity Plus Foundation's work with Headwaters Housing Development Corporation on financial education and homeownership initiatives, and participation in Special Olympics Minnesota funding campaigns.

The credit union is genuinely limited by standard membership eligibility requirements—not everyone qualifies automatically. While the website emphasizes competitive rates and lower fees than "typical banks," specific rate comparisons are not provided on the homepage. Geographic focus on Minnesota members may limit accessibility for those outside the service area. The organization appears well-established and operationally sound, making it suitable for eligible Minnesota residents seeking member-owned banking with community values.
services: Membership Savings accounts with $10 minimum balance
Checking accounts with digital banking and mobile app access
Visa Premier Select Rewards credit cards
MyPlus Rewards program with automatic enrollment
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance with closing cost credits
External account balance aggregation and spending analysis
Card management via digital wallet with replacement and control features
pros: Automatic enrollment in MyPlus Rewards program earning points on everyday debit and credit card spending
Not-for-profit structure returns majority of profits to member-owners rather than external shareholders
Free credit monitoring included with digital banking membership
Multi-factor authentication and security alerts for account protection
Current mortgage promotion: $1,000 credit toward closing costs through May 16, 2026
Low $10 minimum balance to establish membership and ownership share
Transparent, simplified fee structure explicitly marketed as having no surprises
cons: Membership eligibility restricted by common bond requirements—not universally available to all applicants
Geographic limitation to Minnesota service area may exclude out-of-state applicants
Specific interest rates and fee schedules not displayed on main website—requires further inquiry
Limited information about business banking or commercial lending services on consumer-facing pages
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced financial planning tools beyond basic mortgages and loans
best_for: Minnesota residents seeking member-owned banking with community values and profit-sharing benefits
Individuals eligible through employment, residence, education, or family connections to existing members
Homebuyers and refinancers in Minnesota looking to reduce closing costs on mortgages
Consumers prioritizing transparent fees and multi-factor security over large bank convenience
diagnosis: Affinity Plus is best for Minnesota residents who qualify for membership and value a not-for-profit banking structure where profits return to member-owners. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—common bond requirements (location, employment, education, or family connection) may exclude many applicants, and service is geographically limited to Minnesota.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
